student_query: So high acidity means the wine is lower quality?

matched_misconception.misconception: High acidity in a wine means the wine is low quality or unpleasant.
matched_misconception.why_incorrect: Acidity is a structural component, not a quality indicator in isolation. High acidity in a cool-climate Riesling or Champagne is not a defect — it provides freshness, food-pairing versatility, and the backbone for long-term ageing. Whether acidity is pleasant depends on its balance with fruit intensity, residual sugar, and other wine components. An unbalanced high-acid wine may be unpleasant, but the acidity itself is not the cause of poor quality.
matched_misconception.corrected_understanding: High acidity can be a hallmark of quality in cool-climate wines. Its contribution to wine quality depends on whether it is balanced by other components — particularly fruit concentration, sweetness, and alcohol.
detection_signals: This wine is too acidic so it must be poor quality
